seoTitle: St Aubyn vs St Helens — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between St Aubyn and St Helens. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: St Aubyn vs St Helens: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Aubyn and St Helens.
heading: St Aubyn vs St Helens — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Aubyn, Queensland and St Helens, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Helens has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to St Aubyn (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Aubyn or St Helens safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Aubyn has a flood risk score of 48/100, while St Helens scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Aubyn scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. St Helens scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Aubyn or St Helens?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Helens has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.67% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
